item.title: NYT Strands #825 Hints and Answers for Today, June 6, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #825 for Friday, June 6, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 6, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #825 are all terms related to weddings.

General Strands Hints for June 6

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Promises two partners make to each other during their wedding.
  • Hint 2: Holy ____.
  • Hint 3: Partnership two individuals enter after their wedding.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #825 for June 6, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 5, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • TALK
  • TALKS
  • LOCK
  • LOCKS
  • RANT

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #825 On June 6, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • MATRIMONY
  • MARRIAGE
  • VOWS
  • NUPTIALS
  • WEDLOCK
  • SPANAGRAM: TYINGTHEKNOT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #825?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 7 will be Strands Game #826.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG No Longer Moving Forward
item.content:encoded:
Paramount Games Studio executive comments on game projects no longer in development related to its IPs.

Paramount Games Studio was recently formed and announced its first project, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin, at Summer Games Fest. Subsequently, a studio representative named TMNT, SpongeBob SquarePants, Star Trek, and Avatar: The Last Airbender as the studio’s core four IPs.

Paramount Confirms Avatar RPG and Original Ronin Project Cancellation

In an interview with Game File, Shawn Kittelsen, the head of creative at the new studio, confirmed that the previously announced Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG was no longer being developed by the time the studio was formed. The project was also being developed by Saber Interactive and was set to be part of the Avatar Legends Universe. At the time, it was touted as “the biggest video game in franchise history.”

Kittelsen did state that the IP is important enough that a similar project could arise in the future.

“That particular project wasn’t in production when we started this new division,” Kittelsen said. “The Airbender franchise is a high priority for us, so given the right team and opportunity, we would love to bring Aang’s world to life in a AAA game.”

Also, it was confirmed that The Last Ronin is not a continuation of a previously announced project of the same name by THQ Nordic and Black Forest Games. That game is no longer being developed either.

“On the face of it, the pairing of Platinum and The Last Ronin made a lot of sense just from a genre fit standpoint. But when we had the first conversation, the first pitch, it was about so much more than what I expected,” he said.

Insider Gaming will continue to share more news about the future of The Last Ronin whenever more details get shared. Check out everything announced during Summer Games Fest here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Little Nightmares 3 ‘The Backstage’ DLC Coming Next Week
item.content:encoded:
The Backstage will soon be available in Little Nightmares 3. The new DLC expansion drops next week.

During the Future Games Show Summer Showcase 2026, it was revealed that The Backstage DLC will drop on Friday, June 12, 2026. The trailer shown revealed that the expansion will take players “to the underbelly of The Carnevale.” This is the first of two additional story DLCs included in the Secrets of Spiral Expansion Pass.

“This additional tale enriches the experience and lets you uncover hidden secrets, new perspectives, and unexpected horrors,” the default explanation of the chapter reads.

Little Nightmares Franchise Information

Little Nightmares 3 was released in October 2025. It was the first game in the franchise developed by Supermassive Games and the lowest-scoring of its trilogy on Metacritic. The previous two games were developed by Tarsier Studios, but all titles have been published by Bandai Namco. At least one more DLC is expected to be added to the game, which will also allow players to dive deeper into The Spiral. That is expected between October and December. You can check out our review of Little Nightmares 3 here.

In April, a VR game in the franchise was released. Learn more about Little Nightmares VR: Altered Echoes here. Also, in other franchise-related news, the former developers of Little Nightmares are now working on a game that merges twin-stick shooters with Metroidvanias. Learn more here.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Halloween: The Game Reveals Release Date and Pre-Order Bonuses

Pre-orders for the forthcoming Halloween game are now live, and the game launches this September.

Monster Hunter Wilds Confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2

Nintendo Switch 2 continues to grow its library with the official addition of Monster Hunter Wilds from Capcom. The game is coming soon.

Call of Duty: DMZ Has ‘Rewarding PvP’ Mechanics Backed by a Bounty System

Call of Duty: DMZ will have a full-fledged PvP ecosystem backed by leaderboards, a bounty system, and intel-based wanted level missions.

COD DMZ To Feature ‘Living Extraction World’ That Takes Players to Russia and North Korea

Call of Duty: DMZ will feature a rough, harsh, and fractured extraction world that spans several countries, Activision has revealed.
